facts.current_title: The technical writer's survival kit for 2026 (and beyond)

assessment.problem: Highest-volume gap on the site (120 impressions) but an INTENT MISMATCH: the query is 'technical writing books 2026' and the post has no books or reading-list section. A title that promises books would be clickbait that bounces.
assessment.recommendation: Do NOT add 'books' to the title. Either leave the title as-is or route this to keyword-gap/content-decay to add a real 'best technical writing books for 2026' section, which would satisfy the intent and earn the click honestly.
assessment.reasoning: Search Console surfaced this because of keyword overlap ('technical writing ... 2026'), not because the page answers the query. Metadata can't fix a content gap; faking it trades a 0% CTR for a high bounce.
evidence: post body has 0 books/reading-list content (grep confirmed)
intent = book recommendations; page = skills/career guide

options.title: (no metadata change recommended)
options.description: Content gap, not a snippet gap. Best handled by adding a books section (keyword-gap / content-decay), not a title rewrite.

assessment.recommendation: Apply one of 5 title/description options manually, OR point me at the gitfortechnicalwriters repo and I will write the front matter and verify.
